title: Air-fryer asparagus
subtitle: Tender spears with charred tips in 8 minutes
excerpt: Asparagus spears tossed in olive oil and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 6 to 8 minutes. The tips char before the stems toughen, producing a result that is better than boiling or steaming and comparable to a very hot griddle pan without any need for attention.
sourceNotes: British asparagus season runs from late April to late June. English asparagus from the Vale of Evesham and Suffolk is considered among the best in Europe. Outside the British season, asparagus is imported from Peru year-round. The air fryer produces a better result than boiling (which waterloggs the spears) and is faster than oven roasting.

content.text: Snap, do not cut, the woody base. Asparagus spears have a natural break point where the tender stem meets the tough woody base. Hold one end in each hand and bend the spear: it snaps exactly at this point. Cutting with a knife removes a fixed length and either wastes good stem or leaves woody fibres on the plate.

Snap the woody bases from the {{asparagus}} spears. Toss with {{olive-oil}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Spread the spears in the basket in a single layer, tips all facing the same direction. Cook at 200°C for 6 minutes for thin spears or 8 minutes for thick ones, until the tips are lightly charred and the stems bend slightly without snapping. Sprinkle with {{parmesan}} and serve immediately with a squeeze of {{lemon}}.

items.fix: Place the tips pointing toward the centre of the basket away from the heating element if possible. Very thin spears cook in 4 to 5 minutes. Check at 5 minutes and remove as soon as the stems bend slightly
items.cause: Tips in direct contact with the basket, or spears too thin
items.symptom: Tips burnt and dried out before stems are cooked

items.fix: Asparagus overcooks very quickly. Remove at the first sign of the stems bending: 6 minutes is the maximum for most spears. Grey asparagus has been cooked for too long and the chlorophyll has broken down
items.symptom: Spears floppy and grey rather than firm and bright green

items.fix: For very thick asparagus, peel the lower half of the stem with a peeler. Asparagus bought at the end of the season has a tougher stem than early-season spears
items.cause: Thick main stem not peeled, or spears very mature
items.symptom: Woody texture throughout despite snapping off the base

content.text: Air-fryer asparagus is a fast side dish for almost any spring or summer dinner. Serve alongside a salmon fillet, draped over a soft-boiled egg with hollandaise, or with Parmesan and a squeeze of lemon as a starter. During British asparagus season (late April to June) this is the fastest and best cooking method available.
